summary: DOP's own reporting attributes the rise in juveniles arrested while on probation, as a share of all NYPD arrests, to a broader increase in youth arrests citywide and to a growing proportion of youth placed on probation for felony-level charges — a group statistically more likely to be rearrested. DOP says it has responded by refining supervision strategies to focus resources on youth assessed as being at higher risk of reoffending.

sources.label: Department of Probation, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report
